completeness_goals.goal: The submission is a genuine, working implementation of the requested changes to the repository source — not a stub, no-op, or gaming artifact. It must FAIL this floor if it exhibits any of: (a) a zero-edit submission, a diff whose only content is a captured-tree banner such as a line reading '=== repo/ ===', or a diff that only edits changelog/documentation/markdown files without any real source-code change (the requested source work — the skill-loader behavior change and/or the model-catalog model addition — is entirely absent); (b) git-operations exfiltration that fakes progress instead of editing files (e.g. `git checkout main`, `git reset --hard origin/...`, restoring files from another ref); (c) a comment-only or whitespace-only diff that changes no behavior; (d) edits confined to temporary, scratch, or unmerged locations (e.g. /tmp, a throwaway branch, a backup copy) rather than the actual package sources; (e) deleting, weakening, or skipping existing tests to force a pass. The skill-loader behavior change in particular must be real source logic, not a hardcoded value or a parsed-but-ignored flag.
completeness_goals.rationale: Anti-gaming floor. Two real model attempts (glm51, opus46) submitted captured-tree banner diffs ('=== pi-mono/ ===') that touched only CHANGELOG.md and never modified the skill loader source — exactly the diff-pad / changelog-only gaming pattern this floor must catch. The verifier likewise hard-codes that a no-op patch scores 0.0.

completeness_goals.goal: The skill-loading / directory-scanning logic is changed so that when it walks a directory tree it honors standard ignore files (such as .gitignore and equivalent ignore-tool ignore files) via the same ignore-matching infrastructure already used by the repository's other loaders (the shared `ignore` package), rather than via an ad-hoc hardcoded directory blacklist. As a result it does NOT surface skills found inside dependency, virtual-environment, and cache directories that those ignore files exclude (e.g. Python virtualenvs, __pycache__, site-packages), while still discovering genuine skills outside those directories. The exclusion must come from real loader logic in the diff that actually consumes ignore files — not a stub, a hardcoded constant the loader ignores, a commented-out branch, a literal directory-name blacklist that bypasses the ignore infrastructure the user asked to reuse, or hardcoding the excluded directory names directly into the ignore matcher instead of reading them from the discovered ignore files.
completeness_goals.rationale: Turns 1-4 are the heart of the session: T1 'don't we already have infra in other loaders that use the ignore package? shouldn't we be using that here as ewll?', T3 'why do we have the skill loader in skills.ts still...', T4 'ok, can we fix up skills.ts then to work like the resolver in package manager?'. The golden patch wires the `ignore` package into the skill loader so venv/__pycache__/site-packages are skipped; the test's heaviest behavioral gate verifies exactly this.

completeness_goals.goal: A new model entry for the GPT-5.3 Codex model is added to the model-catalog generation source, configured with specifications equivalent to the existing GPT-5.2 Codex entry (same API style, context window, token limits, and analogous cost/capability fields), and the new model is restricted so it is only offered under the OpenAI Codex provider (not the general API provider). The entry must be a real catalog addition that the generator emits, not a commented-out block, a TODO, or a value the generator never reads.
completeness_goals.rationale: T9 'can you hardcode a gpt-5.3-codex model in @packages/ai/scripts/generate-models.ts ... same specs as gpt-5.2-codex' and the explicit correction in Turn 11 'gpt-5.3-codex should only be available for openai-codex provider, doesn't work with the api atm'. The golden patch adds the gpt-5.3-codex entry with provider 'openai-codex' and codex specs mirroring gpt-5.2-codex. The provider restriction is a multi-turn correction that must be captured.

completeness_goals.goal: Per-change changelog entries are added under the current pending-release section (the Unreleased section, or equivalently a freshly-cut 0.52.0 release section if the agent has already cut the release) recording that the new GPT-5.3 Codex model and the Claude Opus 4.6 model are now available, placed in the appropriate change-type section and following the repository's changelog conventions for new additions. Because these model additions are end-user-visible, they are recorded in the package whose source actually changed (the ai/model-catalog package) and duplicated into the user-facing package's changelog (the coding-agent package) per the repository's cross-package duplication convention, or at minimum are present in the user-facing changelog. The entries must reflect the actual changes (including that GPT-5.3 Codex is Codex/OpenAI-Codex-provider only) and must be substantive text, not placeholder or empty bullets.
completeness_goals.rationale: T10 'add a changelog entry that this and opus 4.6 are now available', refined by Turn 11's provider restriction. The golden patch adds 'Added Claude Opus 4.6 model' and 'Added GPT-5.3 Codex model (OpenAI Codex provider only)' to the pending-release Added section of the changelog(s).

completeness_goals.goal: A New Features section is introduced at the top of the user-facing package's pending-release changelog section (the Unreleased section, or a freshly-cut 0.52.0 release section if the release has already been cut) summarizing the newly added end-user-visible capabilities — at minimum Claude Opus 4.6 support and GPT-5.3 Codex support — written as real, descriptive feature entries (linking to relevant docs where the convention calls for it) rather than empty or placeholder bullets. This higher-level summary is distinct from the per-change technical changelog lines and must actually be present in the source.
completeness_goals.rationale: T7's audit process step 6 ('Insert a New Features section at the start of [Unreleased] in packages/coding-agent/CHANGELOG.md'), driven to completion by Turn 12 'we talk about new features, basically just opus 4.6 and codex 5.3 support', Turn 13 'continue', Turn 14 'yes add to top of list'. The golden patch adds a '### New Features' block listing Opus 4.6 and GPT-5.3 Codex support at the top of the coding-agent Unreleased section.

completeness_goals.goal: The requested audit of changelog coverage for prior un-recorded commits is acted on: changelog entries that were missing for recent end-user-facing changes (for example previously-merged work such as SSH-URL support for git packages and the model-selector display improvement) are added to the appropriate package's pending-release changelog section (the Unreleased section, or a freshly-cut 0.52.0 release section if the release has already been cut), following the repository's attribution format for external versus internal contributions (PR/issue link, and author handle for external contributions). The added entries must be substantive, correctly attributed lines reflecting real prior changes, not placeholder bullets, and not merely a textual report with no changelog edits made.
completeness_goals.rationale: T7 explicitly instructs a full changelog audit: 'Audit changelog entries for all commits since the last release ... Verify a changelog entry exists ... Add any missing entries directly', with an attribution format reference for external contributions. The golden patch reflects this by adding previously-missing Unreleased entries (e.g. 'Added SSH URL support for git packages ([#1287] by [@markusn])', 'Model selectors now display the selected model name ([#1275] by [@haoqixu])', and a Fixed entry attributed to [@Gurpartap]) — durable workspace edits distinct from the new-model entries in goal_3/goal_4.

completeness_goals.goal: The new minor release version 0.52.0 is finalized in the workspace, not merely discussed: the pending-release changelog content is rolled into a 0.52.0 release entry (e.g. the '## [Unreleased]' heading in the affected changelog(s) is converted to a '## [0.52.0]' release heading, conventionally dated and leaving the new model/feature entries under it) and/or the relevant package version field(s) are bumped to 0.52.0. This must be a concrete edit to changelog and/or package-version source reflecting the cut release, not a textual claim that the release was cut with no corresponding file change.
completeness_goals.rationale: Turn 15 'oki, cut a new minor release 0.52.0 it is, right?' and Turn 16 '0.52.0' confirm the minor-release decision. Cutting the release is a durable workspace action — promoting the Unreleased section to a 0.52.0 heading and/or bumping package version fields — distinct from the changelog-content goals, so it warrants its own (small) goal.

decomposition_notes: Turn 0's verbatim ask is a structured PR review, but the session immediately pivoted (T1) into reusing existing ignore infrastructure and rewriting the skill loader, then later into adding two new models plus changelog/new-features bookkeeping and a release. The golden patch is a messages-replay capturing the durable workspace changes: skills.ts ignore-matcher fix, two model-catalog additions, and the corresponding changelog/New-Features edits. The headline weight goes to the skill-loader behavior (goal_1, the most-discussed and most-tested change). Model-catalog additions with the codex-only provider correction (goal_2) and three changelog surfaces cover the second half of the session: goal_3 per-change entries (with cross-package duplication into the user-facing changelog per T7's duplication rule), goal_4 the New Features summary, and goal_5 the audit of previously-missing entries for prior merged commits (T7's 'add any missing entries directly' with external-contribution attribution). The 0.52.0 minor-release cut (Turn 15-16 '0.52.0') IS a durable workspace action — promoting the pending Unreleased section to a 0.52.0 heading and/or bumping package version fields — so it is captured by goal_6. Process-only turns are intentionally excluded: the structured-review prose (Turn 0) and the test/coverage/commit/push turns (Turns 4-6, 8, 13) leave no durable, behaviorally-verifiable workspace artifact in the golden patch. Weights rank by user-importance and time spent, deliberately NOT mirroring the test_manifest F2P weights (which put only 0.20 on the skill loader and the rest on review-prose heuristics). Both observed model patches were changelog-only captured-tree artifacts, calibrating goal_0's anti-gaming patterns.
